news.title: Five stars of the World Cup who could get a big move this summer!
news.lede: Every four years, football’s global showpiece provides the perfect platform for players to boost their career trajectory and attract some of the world’s biggest clubs. From James Rodriguez’s high-profile move to Real Madrid in 2014 to Enzo Fernandez’s £107 million switch to Chelsea in 2023, many players have secured career-defining transfers on the back of impressive performances on the World Cup stage. The 2026 edition was no different, and here at Flashscore, we’ve picked out five World Cup stars who could be on the move ahead of the 2026/27 season. Bouaddi underlined his status as one of the most promising young players in Europe with a terrific tournament in the heart of Morocco’s midfield.

news.title: How World Cup 2026 proved football has remarkable ability to cure all ills
news.lede: The trophy safely on display at the Spanish FA headquarters, England’s wounds no longer so raw, and the annual football calendar now into its miniscule vacuum, you’re left to reflect on the past six weeks and ponder whether the 2026 edition in the United States, Canada and Mexico was a resounding success or an unmitigated flop. This one really could have gone either way. The expansion from 32 teams to 48 had the potential for greater drama but also the risk of suffocating, of making the World Cup the one thing it should never be: tedious. Would North America buy into soccer in a way we hadn’t seen at USA 1994?

news.title: Lionel Messi and his insane list of FIFA World Cup records
news.lede: Lionel Messi will leave behind an incredible legacy after playing what was very likely his last ever match in the FIFA World Cup. The Argentina captain was not able to lead the national team to glory again, as La Albiceleste lost 1-0 to Spain after extra-time last Sunday. But Messi may well go down as the greatest player in the competition’s history after setting an unfathomable number of records this summer and throughout his career. The only player who can possibly come close to knocking the former Barcelona star off his perch is Kylian Mbappe, who overtook Messi as the all-time leading goalscorer in World Cup with his 22nd goal in the third-place play-off.

news.title: World Cup 2026: Spain midfielder says Argentina players should not be punished by FIFA after post-match scuffle
news.lede: Tensions boiled over following Spain's 1-0 win over Argentina in the World Cup final. Moments after the match ended, Argentina's Leandro Paredes grabbed Spain's Eric García by the throat. After shoving García to the ground, Paredes turned his attention to Spain's Gavi. A day later, FIFA announced it would investigate Argentina for its actions following the loss.

news.title: New faces, gulfs in class and flawed drama: how 48 teams changed the World Cup
news.lede: The first 48-team World Cup had its positives and negatives – do we need to brace for a 64-team future? It was less than 12 months after Gianni Infantino succeeded Sepp Blatter as president that Fifa announced the expansion of the men’s World Cup from 32 to 48 teams. Diego Maradona said he was “delighted by Gianni’s initiative” because it allowed each country to dream of reaching the tournament, renewing their passion for football. Joachim Löw, then in charge of world champions Germany, said: “I don’t think it’s a good idea to dilute the sporting value … In the long term, the quality suffers.”

news.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
news.lede: Fight breaks out between Spain and Argentina players at full-time. Spain midfielder Gavi does not believe Argentina's Leandro Paredes should be suspended for his part in the confrontation following the World Cup final. Argentina players Paredes, Nahuel Molina and Thiago Almada plus assistant coach Roberto Ayala all appeared to be involved in the fracas on the pitch as Spain celebrated their 1-0 win. Paredes seemed to push Spain's Eric Garcia by the throat and unused substitute Gavi tried to intervene but was forced to the ground by Almada.

news.title: World Cup 2030 (very early) power rankings: who will challenge Spain at top?
news.lede: The next men’s World Cup is still four years away, but we take a look at the teams with the firmest foundations who should be primed to contend. The 2026 World Cup provided many an esteemed veteran with what could be their international swansongs, with golden greats Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, Luka Modric, Neymar, James Rodríguez and Guillermo Ochoa all making teary exits as their tournament ended. As we turn ahead to the 2030 World Cup, any player 32 or older will be sweating their selection status and changing their daily routines to stay sharp against inevitable aging. For these power rankings, we’re overvaluing sure things.

news.title: Shakira Leads FIFA’s Historic First World Cup Final Halftime Show
news.lede: Shakira made history on July 19 by headlining FIFA’s first-ever World Cup final halftime show, placing Colombia at the center of one of football’s biggest global spectacles. Performing at MetLife Stadium alongside international stars including Madonna, BTS, and Justin Bieber, the Colombian singer delivered “Dai Dai,” the official anthem of FIFA’s Global Education Fund, during a landmark production that combined music, football, and philanthropy. The performance marked a historic first for the men’s FIFA World Cup, introducing a Super Bowl-style halftime show designed to celebrate both the sport and its growing cultural influence around the world.
